mapping.a_element: rotor-router model as a deterministic analogue of random walk
mapping.b_element: Eulerian walkers model with fixed arrow-flipping rules

mapping.a_element: deterministic growth process (rotor-router aggregation) forming a Euclidean ball
mapping.b_element: particle addition process leading to a critical steady state

mapping.a_element: abelian sandpile model (mentioned as a related model)
mapping.b_element: abelian group generated by particle addition operators in the Eulerian walkers model

mapping.a_element: asymptotic shape (Euclidean ball) with bounded radius error
mapping.b_element: critical steady state in the Eulerian walkers model

brief_justification: Both papers involve deterministic, rule-based processes on lattices that exhibit abelian group structures (rotor-router/divisible sandpile in A, Eulerian walkers in B). The underlying machinery is the same: abelian distributed dynamics with local update rules leading to emergent global properties (asymptotic shape in A, critical steady state in B).
